what_it_does: A local environment-variable file holding the 'boundary' principal credentials used to authenticate against a local Supabase instance for the company-channel. It defines a service-account email and password, the local Supabase URL, the password-grant token endpoint, and an anon JWT key.
observations: Header comment: 'LOCAL boundary principal cred — OUT OF CHANNEL, never paste. fork's boundary auths as this.'
COMPANY_CHANNEL_SA_PASSWORD is a plaintext password value
COMPANY_CHANNEL_TOKEN_ENDPOINT uses grant_type=password against the local auth endpoint
COMPANY_CHANNEL_ANON_KEY is a JWT (HS256, iss supabase-demo, role anon, exp 1983812996)
standouts: Contains a live plaintext service-account password and an anon JWT
Self-labels as a secret that should never be pasted / is out-of-channel
concerns: Plaintext credentials checked into a build-prep directory under the repo path; the file itself warns it is a secret
Anon JWT 'iss' is 'supabase-demo' (default local/demo issuer), suggesting a development/demo Supabase stack
notes: A local secrets dotfile giving the fork's 'boundary' principal its company-channel Supabase login against a 127.0.0.1 dev instance.
questions: Is this file gitignored, or is it actually tracked despite the 'never paste' warning?
What is 'fork's boundary' — which process/principal consumes these vars?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (config/secrets file; values match its stated role as the boundary principal credential)
apparent_themes: Local-only authentication boundary for the channel
intention_signals: Trying to give a 'boundary' principal a defined local identity so the fork can authenticate to the local company-channel Supabase without hardcoding creds in code.
novelty: The named 'boundary' principal concept with a dedicated dev credential file.
contribution: Contributes the local boundary-principal credential set (email/password/URL/token-endpoint/anon-key) for authenticating to the local company-channel Supabase.
summary_for_embedding: Local env dotfile with the boundary service-account credentials, Supabase URL, token endpoint, and anon JWT for the company-channel.
intention_for_embedding: To authenticate the fork's boundary principal against the local company-channel Supabase instance.
